title: Isotrema grande
taxonomy: Isotrema grande is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. The genus Isotrema is part of the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which is a group of flowering plants known as milkweeds. Isotrema grande is native to the western United States and is often found in dry, open areas. This plant is a perennial herb, and its classification as a member of the Asclepiadoideae subfamily suggests that it has characteristics such as milky latex and paired leaves. Further studies may be necessary to confirm Isotrema grande's classification and its relationships to other plants within the Apocynaceae family.
